task: Author the runtime adapters that connect the pure general-browser-agent loop (services/general-browser-agent.js, which exports runBrowserGoal) to a real Puppeteer browser session and a cheapest-tier LLM decider. These adapters are the injected side-effect functions runBrowserGoal expects.
spec:
ES module. Add a JSDoc header with '@ssot docs/products/tc-service/PRODUCT_HOME.md'. Import nothing external except optionally the sibling loop is NOT imported here. Export exactly these named functions:

1) export async function observePage(session, opts = {}) — session is the object returned by services/browser-agent.js createSession(): it has session.page (a Puppeteer Page), and helpers currentUrl(), pageText(). Return an observation object: { url, title, text, elements }. url = await session.currentUrl() (fallback session.page.url()). title = await session.page.title(). text = (await session.pageText()) truncated to opts.maxTextChars || 4000 chars. elements = result of session.page.evaluate over interactive elements (a, button, input, select, textarea, [role=button]) that are visible: for each collect { tag, type, name, id, text (trimmed innerText/placeholder/value/aria-label, max 80 chars), selector }. selector should prefer #id, else a CSS path using tag+nth-of-type. Cap elements at opts.maxElements || 40. Wrap the evaluate in try/catch and return elements: [] on failure. Never throw.

2) export function formatObservation(observation, goal) — pure. Return a compact plain-text prompt string describing the goal and the current page (url, title, first 1500 chars of text, and a numbered list of elements with their index, tag, text, and selector). Keep under ~2500 chars.

3) export function parseModelAction(text) — pure. The model returns an action as JSON (possibly inside prose or a code fence). Extract the FIRST valid JSON object and return it as a plain object. If none parses, return { type: 'give_up', reason: 'unparseable_model_output' }. Never throw.

4) export function makeDecider({ callModel, tiers }) — returns an async function decideAction({ goal, observation, history }). It builds the prompt with formatObservation, appends instructions to reply ONLY with a JSON action of the form {\"type\":\"navigate|click|type|wait|done|give_up\", ...fields}, then calls callModel(tier, prompt) starting at the cheapest tier (tiers[0]); on thrown error or empty output it escalates to the next tier. Returns parseModelAction(output). If all tiers fail, return { type: 'give_up', reason: 'all_tiers_failed' }.

5) export function makeEvidenceVerifier({ mustContain = [], mustHaveSelector = [] }) — returns an async function verifyGoal({ goal, observation }). reached is true ONLY IF every string in mustContain is present (case-insensitive) in observation.text AND every selector in mustHaveSelector matches some element in observation.elements (by selector or by text). Return { reached, evidence: { matchedText, matchedSelectors, url: observation.url } }. Pure aside from reading the passed observation. Never fabricate — only report what is present.

6) export function makeAccountConfirmer({ expectSiteHost = null, expectAccountText = null }) — returns an async function confirmContext({ observation }). ok is true only if (expectSiteHost is null OR observation.url includes expectSiteHost) AND (expectAccountText is null OR observation.text includes expectAccountText). Return { ok, reason: ok ? undefined : 'context_mismatch' }.

7) export async function executeAction(session, action) — perform the action against the session. navigate: await session.navigate(action.url). click: await session.click(action.selector). type: await session.fill(action.selector, action.text). wait: await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, Math.min(action.ms || 1000, 5000))). For done/give_up: return { ok: true, noop: true }. Return { ok: true } on success, { ok: false, error } on failure (catch, never throw). Assume session has navigate(url), click(selector), fill(selector, text) methods.

All functions must be robust: catch errors in the async I/O ones and return structured results rather than throwing. Do not console.log.
